title: Street Cries.
content:
Oft seems the Time a market-town
    Where many merchant-spirits meet
    Who up and down and up and down
    Cry out along the street

    Their needs, as wares; one THUS, one SO:
    Till all the ways are full of sound:
     - But still come rain, and sun, and snow,
    And still the world goes round.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Sidney Lanier, titled "Street Cries.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
